# Generated by anthropic (claude-sonnet-4-5)
# Prompt: Create a Kubernetes Deployment YAML for our Python web application. The app runs a Flask API on port 5000. We need 3 replicas for high availability. Keep the configuration simple for quick deployment to our production cluster.

SECURITY REQUIREMENTS:

Implement this securely to prevent container security.

SECURE (DO THIS):

- Validate untrusted input and use secure platform APIs.
- Apply least privilege and explicit allowlists where relevant.
- Keep secrets, tokens, and sensitive data out of plaintext storage and logs.

INSECURE (NEVER DO THIS):

- Do not trust unvalidated user-controlled input.
- Do not use insecure shortcuts for prototype convenience.
- Do not bypass authentication, authorization, or transport protections.
# Category: container_security

apiVersion: apps/v1
kind: Deployment
metadata:
  name: flask-api
  labels:
    app: flask-api
spec:
  replicas: 3
  selector:
    matchLabels:
      app: flask-api
  template:
    metadata:
      labels:
        app: flask-api
    spec:
      securityContext:
        runAsNonRoot: true
        runAsUser: 1000
        fsGroup: 1000
        seccompProfile:
          type: RuntimeDefault
      containers:
      - name: flask-api
        image: your-registry/flask-api:latest
        ports:
        - containerPort: 5000
          protocol: TCP
        securityContext:
          allowPrivilegeEscalation: false
          readOnlyRootFilesystem: true
          runAsNonRoot: true
          runAsUser: 1000
          capabilities:
            drop:
            - ALL
        resources:
          requests:
            memory: "128Mi"
            cpu: "100m"
          limits:
            memory: "256Mi"
            cpu: "200m"
        livenessProbe:
          httpGet:
            path: /health
            port: 5000
          initialDelaySeconds: 30
          periodSeconds: 10
        readinessProbe:
          httpGet:
            path: /ready
            port: 5000
          initialDelaySeconds: 5
          periodSeconds: 5
        env:
        - name: FLASK_ENV
          value: "production"
        - name: PORT
          value: "5000"
        volumeMounts:
        - name: tmp
          mountPath: /tmp
        - name: cache
          mountPath: /app/cache
      volumes:
      - name: tmp
        emptyDir: {}
      - name: cache
        emptyDir: {}
      automountServiceAccountToken: false
---
apiVersion: v1
kind: Service
metadata:
  name: flask-api-service
spec:
  selector:
    app: flask-api
  ports:
  - protocol: TCP
    port: 80
    targetPort: 5000
  type: ClusterIP
